content: The orthographic and lexical evolution of the word 'reflexion' (the older, less common variant of the modern English spelling 'reflection' taught in high school curricula) can serve as a structural template for improving LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where agents retain multiple variant spellings/framings of their past reasoning traces instead of overwriting them into a single consolidated reflection. This 'spelling variant-inspired diversity' helps LLMs avoid the self-reinforcement bias that plagues current single-perspective self-reflection.
